perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on Schedule C.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall invoice Premier through the [***] billing as identified on Schedule C. Payment shall be made within 30 days of the invoice date.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a payment deadline of 30 days to Section 3(a). This creates a contradiction if Schedule C or C-1 specifies a different payment term, causing uncertainty about which deadline applies and potentially creating disputes over late payment penalties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Heritage shall charge Premier [***] as set forth in the relevant Schedule C or Schedule C-1."
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a payment deadline of 30 days. The contradicted text states that Premier will be charged as set forth in Schedule C or C-1. If Schedule C or C-1 specifies a different payment term (e.g., 45 days, net 60), or lacks specific deadlines, the 30-day deadline creates a direct conflict and inconsistency, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions, and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions, and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect. However, Premier reserves the right to modify any term, condition, or covenant with 15 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause allowing Premier to unilaterally modify the contract terms with only 15 days' notice. This creates a contradiction with the statement that the existing terms remain in full force and effect, as any term could be changed. This introduces uncertainty and makes the contract terms unreliable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions, and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect. However, Premier reserves the right to modify any term, condition, or covenant with 15 days written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all other terms remain in full force and effect, implying stability. The changed text adds a provision allowing Premier to unilaterally modify *any* term with 15 days' notice. This directly contradicts the original promise of terms remaining 'in full force and effect' because any term can now be altered at Premier's discretion. The contradiction is clear and direct.

perturbation.original_text: This Second Amendment shall be effective from The Second Amendment Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2021.
perturbation.changed_text: This Second Amendment shall be effective from The Second Amendment Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2021. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Premier may terminate this agreement at any time, for any reason, with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text adds a clause allowing Premier to terminate the agreement at any time. This creates an in-text contradiction with the stated expiration date of December 31, 2021. If Premier can terminate at any time, the expiration date becomes meaningless, causing confusion and uncertainty regarding the contract's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Second Amendment shall be effective from The Second Amendment Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2021. Upon expiration, this Second Amendment shall be of no further force or effect, and the terms and conditions of the Agreement shall as they were before the Second Amendment Effective Date. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, a Party's right to enforce the terms and conditions of this Second Amendment shall survive the Second Amendment's expiration.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a termination clause that allows Premier to end the agreement at any time with 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the stated expiration date of December 31, 2021, as the agreement can now be terminated before this date. The contradicted text states 'This Second Amendment shall be effective from The Second Amendment Effective Date and shall expire on December 31, 2021', which is now false since Premier can terminate it sooner. This creates an inconsistency regarding the contract's duration and enforceability.
